LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-17-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITH PREJUDICE. FIRST, DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE SAFE HARBOR PROVISION OF CCP 128.7(C)(1). SECOND, THE MOTION DOES NOT DESCRIBE IMPROPER, HARASSING, OR FRIVOLOUS CONDUCT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 128.7(B). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RCE)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RE: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE OR MODIFY JUDGEMENT TO THE CLAIMS IN THE COMPLAINT 1. THIS COURT IS PROPER BECAUSE A DEFENDANT LIVES HERE 2. PERPETUAL CLAIMS OF EXEMPTION 3. HARASSMENT 4. INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS 5. PRAYER FOR RELIEF. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. DEFECTIVE PROOF OF SERVICE. MOVANT ADDRESSED THE MOTION TO THE CLIENT (BANK OF AMERICA) INSTEAD OF THE CLIENT'S COUNSEL (DONALD SHERRILL, ESQ. OF HUNT & HENRIQUES, LLP). JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

For Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petitioners Motion For Attorney'S Fees; Declarations; Exhibits
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case No. CGC-19-581238, Bank of America vs. Austin Carrington Carmichael.

Appearances via Zoom:
Kurtiss Jacobs, Esq., Tel. 408.414.0369, appearing for plaintiff Bank of America;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL's Motion For Attorney's Fees.

The matter is not contested. No other appearance in this matter. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Austin Carrington Carmichael's motion for attorney's fees is denied with prejudice. Carmichael cannot recover attorney's fees and costs as he is not an attorney. (Trope v. Katz (1995) 11 Cal.4th 275, 285.) "[I]t is clear that when it enacted section 1717 the Legislature did not intend to allow doctors, architects, painters, or any other nonattorneys to receive compensation for the valuable time they spend litigating a contract matter on their own behalf." (Id.)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declarations; Exhibits
Off Calendar May-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. KURTISS JACOBS, Esq. of Hunt & Henriques, LLP (tel: 408-414-0369) for Plaintiff.
Defendant AUSTIN CARRINGTON CARMICHAEL, in pro per present (tel: 415-260-8130).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL's Motion For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied with prejudice. First, defendant failed to comply with the safe harbor provision of CCP 128.7(c)(1). Second, the motion does not describe improper, harassing, or frivolous conduct within the meaning of CCP 128.7(b).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
